channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Shell and MAN Collaborate with Rivers State to Boost Industrial Gas Supply
item.description: Shell Nigeria Gas Limited, together with the Manufacturers Association of Nigeria (MAN), don show serious support for Rivers State plan wey dey aim to expand gas supply to industries. Dis effort na pa...

item.title: Global Health Experts Warn AMR Remains a Persistent Threat to Health Systems
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) continues to warn that antimicrobial resistance (AMR) is a serious global health threat, despite ongoing efforts worldwide. In 2019, AMR contributed to nearly 5 mil...

item.title: Life in China During the Early 1920s Through Vintage Photographs
item.description: Life in China during the early 1920s reflected a stark contrast between rapid modernization in urban centers and traditional rural life. In cities like Shanghai and Guangzhou, new technologies such as...

item.title: Why Kenya Keeps Struggling With High Fuel Prices Despite Local Oil
item.description: Kenya's fuel crisis isn’t just about President William Ruto or the current administration. The root cause goes back to the 2013 closure of the Changamwe refinery and long-standing foreign control of t...

item.title: Faisal Shuaibu emerges APC senatorial candidate for Nasarawa West
item.description: Faisal Shuaibu, wey be former chairman of the National Primary Health Care Development Agency (NPHCDA), don secure the All Progressives Congress (APC) senatorial ticket for Nasarawa West. The announce...

item.title: Mako Nishimura: Life Inside and Beyond Japan’s Yakuza
item.description: Mako Nishimura, possibly the only woman to fully join Japan's male-dominated yakuza, lived a life of violence, crime, and eventual redemption. From a troubled childhood marked by strict parenting and ...

item.title: BARMM Peace Adviser Urges Calm and Unity Ahead of Historic September Elections

item.title: CDSCO clarifies definition and safe use of cosmetics in India
item.description: The Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO) of India has issued a clarification regarding what constitutes cosmetic products under the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940. According to the notic...

item.title: Rivers State Government Appreciates Anglican Church for Contributions to Peace and Community Development
item.description: The Rivers State Government don show high respect and appreciation for the Anglican Church of Nigeria for the role wey dem dey play for development of Rivers State. Governor Siminalayi Fubara yan say ...

item.title: The Role of Fruits and Vegetables in Supporting Mental Health Across Lifespan
item.description: Mental health is a critical aspect of overall well-being, influenced by genetics, environment, social factors, and lifestyle habits, including diet. Recent research highlights that diets rich in fruit...

item.title: Woman taken into custody over newborn death investigation
item.description: In Tanzania, police have taken a mother into custody over allegations that she was involved in the death of her newborn baby. Authorities are investigating the circumstances surrounding the tragic inc...

item.title: Kenyan Bride Leads Worship at Her Wedding, Captures Social Media Attention
item.description: On her wedding day, Liz Mungai decided to make her entrance a bit different from the usual. Instead of just smiling and walking down the aisle, she took a microphone and led a full praise and worship ...

item.title: Onoh advises Jonathan against 2027 presidential run due to political challenges
item.description: Former President Goodluck Jonathan don dey advised make e no try run for 2027 presidential election again, according to Denge Dr. Josef Onoh, wey be ex-South-East spokesman for President Bola Tinubu. ...

item.title: Jennifer Jason Leigh’s Formative Roles and Career in the 1980s
item.description: Jennifer Jason Leigh, born February 5, 1962, is an American actress whose career began on television in the 1970s and quickly transitioned to film. She gained widespread attention for her breakthrough...

item.title: Morgan Fairchild’s Early Career and Television Roles in the 1970s
item.description: Morgan Fairchild, born Patsy Ann McClenny on February 3, 1950, is a renowned American actress who began her career in the early 1970s. She gained prominence as a glamorous television figure during the...

item.title: Canada-U.S. Trade Talks Stall Amid Tensions and Policy Disagreements
item.description: Trade negotiations between Canada and the United States have reached a stalemate, creating concern among business leaders and politicians. The impasse involves Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and ...

item.title: Kwam 1 releases anthem to celebrate Arsenal’s Premier League triumph
item.description: Fuji legend Wasiu 'Kwam 1' Ayinde don release new celebratory song to hail Arsenal Football Club after dem win Premier League for first time in 22 years. Dem EPL title come after Manchester City draw ...

item.title: High Court continues hearing $195 million graphite mining dispute between Pula Group and ARC
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The High Court’s Commercial Division is currently handling a major $195 million legal case involving Pula Group and African Rainbow Capital (ARC) in Tanzania’s graphite sector. The dis...

item.title: Victoria Beckham showcases radiant skin following facial treatments and discusses fashion business growth
item.description: Victoria Beckham, aged 51, was recently seen enjoying a series of high-end facial treatments at Keren Bartov's clinic in west London, known for catering to celebrity clients such as Gal Gadot and Isla...

item.title: Maina Kageni Faces Loss After Arsenal Wins Premier League
item.description: Radio personality Maina Kageni is counting losses after a bet he made on Arsenal backfired. The Classic 105 host had pledged to pay KSh 1 million should Arsenal win the Premier League, ending their 22...
